revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23128882600|commit triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Center%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high use}} == Description == It is to center elements on a page. See also [[Template:Align]] for more options and flexibility. == Usage == {{See also|Span and div}} {{markup | {{Center|Hello world!}} | {{Center|Hello world!}} }} To do this without a template, simply use the following code:
...
 To center a table, see [[Help:TABLECENTER]]. Notes: * If the markup to be centered includes an equals sign, the markup must be preceded by 1=. Example: {{Center|1=the red one}}. * If the markup to be centered includes a displaying pipe, the pipe must be escaped as {{!}} or |. === Tables === Using {{tlc|left}} / {{tlc|center}} / {{tlc|right}} {| class="wikitable" !colspan="3" style="width:300px"| Header text |- | {{Left|Left}} || {{Center|Center}} || {{Right|Right}} |} Using {{tlc|align|left}} / {{tlc|align|center}} / {{tlc|align|right}} {| class="wikitable" !colspan="3" style="width:300px"| Header text |- | {{align|left|Left}} || {{align|center|Center}} || {{align|right|Right}} |} == Tracking categories == *{{clc|Pages using center with no arguments}} *{{clc|Pages using center with unknown parameters}} == TemplateData == {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Content", "type": "content", "required": true, "description": "Content to be centered" }, "style": { "label": "Custom CSS", "description": "Optional css styling for the centered text", "type": "string" } }, "description": "A simple template to center-align content in tables and other places. Be aware that the center class is applied to this template, which means every element within the template has its left and right margins set to auto. This may harm some templates." } == See also == * {{tl|Center block}} * {{tl|align}} * {{tl|right}}, {{tl|left}} and {{tl|justify}} * {{tl|Image frame}} * {{tl|Table alignment}} - to align whole columns in tables. {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Positioning templates]] [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia XHTML tag-replacing templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
